title: Bloq Quantum Partners with MIUUL to Expand Quantum Machine Learning in Turkey - Quantum Computing Report
gist: Kerala, India-based Bloq Quantum entered the Turkish market via a training partnership with tech-skilling firm MIUUL, embedding its low-code quantum machine learning platform into MIUUL's bootcamp curriculum to speed classical ML engineers' transition into hybrid quantum algorithms.

themes.theme: Quantum software go-to-market via education channels

insights.claim: Bloq Quantum, a Kerala, India-based quantum software provider, is using a training-partner model (with MIUUL in Turkey) as its entry mechanism into new national markets rather than direct enterprise sales.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: Watch for quantum software vendors expanding internationally through skilling/training ecosystems rather than direct sales — a low-capex distribution strategy worth tracking for early market-share signals in emerging quantum hubs.
insights.quote: The collaboration marks Bloq Quantum’s official entry into the Turkish market.

insights.claim: Bloq Quantum claims its low-code 'data-to-deployment' interface lets technical teams build and test hybrid quantum-classical workflows up to 10x faster than traditional manual SDK/cloud-environment setup.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: A 10x claimed dev-speed improvement is a differentiator investors should probe for evidence/benchmarks, since low-code abstraction layers are becoming a competitive vector in quantum software tooling.
insights.quote: Bloq Quantum’s low-code interface allows technical teams to build and test hybrid workflows up to 10x faster

insights.claim: The Bloq-MIUUL program targets producing quantum-ready professionals for financial services, logistics, and data analytics use cases specifically within Turkey's expanding deep-tech sector.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: Signals which verticals (finance, logistics, analytics) quantum vendors expect near-term hybrid QML adoption in, useful for mapping addressable market by sector.
